notes: User opens with '401K bulldozer equipment dedup' failures on auto-config and wants to 'address both issues in the Golden Suite' (auto-config brittleness + OOM/cost blow-ups). Refactors GoldenMatch's auto_configure_df and dedupe_df pipeline. Three architectural concepts emerge: (1) domain-aware auto-config backed by YAML presets (electronics/people/etc.) layered on top of the existing regex-based profiler, (2) an autonomous preflight stage that runs a small 5K-row sample through the full pipeline, measures resources, and auto-downgrades config when projections exceed safety budgets, (3) runtime circuit breakers that cut off the real run when memory/cost/time budgets are exceeded. The existing `auto_configure_df` and `dedupe_df` entry points are being refined rather than replaced — hence `slug_reuse_context: ['auto-configure-df']`. Forbidden slugs are orchestration artifacts (task-list scaffolding, feature-branch creation, memory-note updates) not architectural decisions.
